source_label: Wikipedia ‚Äî Sandworm (Dune): name derived from Arabic shay' khulud, "thing of immortality"
notes: Shai-Hulud is the self-replicating npm supply-chain worm that hit thousands of packages from September 2025 through the "Shai-Hulud 2.0" and "Mini Shai-Hulud" waves, drawing a CISA alert and Microsoft/Unit 42 advisories; it is named for the Dune sandworm. Say it "shy-hoo-LOOD" with stress on the last syllable, from Arabic shay' khulud ("thing of immortality") ‚Äî the reading used in the Dune films and in Frank Herbert's own recorded readings. Cold readers reach for "SHAY hyoo-lud" or "shy-HULL-ud"; a reduced middle vowel ("shy-huh-LOOD") is a valid variant.
